findings.location: save() / line 24 (save.php, referenced from admin.php form action)
findings.reachability: Authenticated administrator-level users only (ADS_TXT_MANAGE_CAPABILITY = edit_ads_txt, assigned only to administrator role). Requires a valid nonce.
findings.justification: save.php line 24 casts the POST field `post_id` to int and passes it as `'ID'` to `wp_insert_post()` without verifying the caller owns or should be allowed to mutate that specific post. Any post ID in the DB can be submitted, causing wp_insert_post to overwrite that post's content and change its post_type to 'adstxt'. Since the capability is administrator-only, this is within WordPress's admin-trusted model, but the intent is to only mutate the plugin's own adstxt/app-adstxt posts.

detection_pattern.description: Catches handlers that cast a user-supplied POST field to int and use it as the WP post ID in wp_insert_post without verifying the caller has edit access to that specific post. An attacker with sufficient capability can overwrite arbitrary posts.
